rows.notes: Clear centered year at top of page; no monetary amounts present.
Clearly written title under the year; no amounts in the row.
Very clear entry at top right. Numbers align cleanly in the three currency columns: 3852 (£), 10 (s), 5 (d).
Description partly abbreviated; monetary columns well aligned. Pence digit slightly faint but reads as '4'.

The word 'Stamps' is clear, but a small isolated '12' mark sits near the amount area and is ambiguous (it could be a note or misalignment from the previous/next entry). Because 12 pence would violate the pence 0-11 rule and alignment is unclear, no amount was assigned.

rows.description: Symm Balance Rectory House & Building attached
rows.notes: Description readable though slightly abbreviated. A clear '505' sits in the pounds column; no shillings/pence visible for this line.
Single clear '50' in the pounds column; description legible.
Another clear '50' in pounds column. The repeated 'do' (ditto) is legible.
Clear '10' in pounds column adjacent to the description.

rows.description: Colby allowance as residing in house shift
rows.notes: Description is somewhat abbreviated/uncertain ('house shift' is interpretation). The amount '40' in pounds column is clear.
Handwritten name is uncertain (read as 'Druse Bill Garsythe'). Two numerals appear; interpreted as £15 and 13s. No pence visible. Slight blotting around the shillings digit reduces confidence.
Three-column group visible and aligned: 11 (pounds), 12 (shillings), 1 (pence). Handwriting for description slightly cramped but readable.

rows.description: Clerk St Michaels Church. for add & trouble during
rows.notes: Short amount '1' in pounds column. The remainder of description runs across the line; faint but interpretable. No shillings/pence visible.
Two numerals aligned in leftmost and middle currency columns: 4 (pounds), 6 (shillings). Pence column blank. Writing is clear.
Three-column amount is clearly written as 46 (£), 16 (s), 4 (d). Description legible.
Two numbers visible and aligned: 6 in pounds column and 9 in shillings column (no pence). The name is slightly abbreviated but readable.
The description is legible but there is no clear amount on the same line. There are several small numerals nearby on following lines; this appears to be a line without its own amount (sub-item).

Description and multiple internal figures present (e.g. '13.4.6' and '4.15') and a larger total at right read as £17 19s 6d. The internal breakdown is faint; total alignment to currency columns is used to extract the amount.
Clear '50' in the pounds column beside the 'Rowles farm' description. Handwriting of the descriptor is legible though slightly abbreviated.
Two numerals present in the left currency columns; interpreted as £18 and 14s. The pence column appears blank for this line. Slight smearing on the shillings digit lowers confidence.
Three-column amount reads clearly as £40 2s 6d. Description includes an ordinal (first half-year) and is readable.
Following-line interest entry clearly shows £48 8s 9d. 'second do' is legible as shorthand for the same item.
Monetary columns show 3, 10, 4 aligned under £, s, d respectively; description is clear.
The descriptive text includes abbreviations and handwriting is a little cramped. Amount reads as £24 5s (no pence visible).

rows.description: Allnutt advanced to be repaired by Bursar

Short description 'C Room' with a clear '3' in the pounds column. No further currency digits on the line.
Two digits appear but are slightly faint; interpreted as £7 and 7s. The pence column is blank. Hand is somewhat cramped which reduces confidence.
Clear three-column amount visible as £8 13s 6d. Description legible.
Amount reads as 5 (pounds), 18 (shillings), 1 (pence) though the shillings digit is slightly crowded; description contains parentheses and is somewhat compressed.
A ditto line ('do') with two aligned numerals read as £5 and 1s. Pence column blank. Clearer digits than previous line.
Another 'do' line with a single '5' visible in pounds column. The line is faint and could represent a small charge; left shillings/pence blank due to lack of clear marks.
A clear '80' appears in the pounds column on this line. Description reads 'Porter cleaning Chapel' though the script is slightly slanted.

rows.description: Griffiths loan of room for chapel fixtures 45?
rows.notes: This appears to be two related lines: the 'Griffiths loan...' description and then a large amount that aligns as £343 18s 2d. The number grouping is very clear and used for this entry.
The phrase 'Room Rent Balance &' is visible and the same clear amount grouping £343 18s 2d is adjacent. It appears that the amount is the room-rent balance noted; alignment is very good.
Amount reads as £7 11s 2d, though the line is compact and the digits are smaller than many above. Description 'Attwood - Ten' is legible.
Two digits visible and aligned; taken as £34 and 15s. The pence column shows no clear digit. Handwriting is slightly faded here.
'Wash. 1859' is readable and the amount to its right is clearly 277 (pounds), 3 (shillings), 3 (pence). Good alignment with vertical ruling lines.
Short description 'Symm Gen. Bill' and amount reads as £10 1s 6d. The pence digit is a little small but legible.
Single surname 'Baxter' with a clear number grouping '34 0 6' visible in the three currency columns. Alignment and digits are distinct.
Name 'Thomas' with two aligned numerals read as £8 and 8s. The pence column is blank; the second digit is faint which reduces confidence.
Surname is legible. Numerals read as £22 and 2s; pence not visible. Slight shadowing from lower page area affects contrast.
Name 'Parker' with a clear three-column amount of £14 5s 3d. Digits are well aligned and readable.
Final name in the vertical list is faint and could be 'Nunney' or similar. There is no separate clear numeric amount on the same line; the following horizontal total line likely concludes the column.
Bottom total line is clear and aligned across the three currency columns as £5810 10s 6d. This is the page total; the horizontal rule above it confirms subtotaling.
